steps.title: Apply the discriminant condition
steps.content:
A quadratic $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$ has no real solutions when its discriminant is negative:
$$b^2 - 4ac < 0$$

Here $a = -2$, $c = -32$, so:
$$b^2 - 4(-2)(-32) < 0$$
$$b^2 - 256 < 0$$
$$b^2 < 256$$

steps.title: Find the largest positive integer $b$
steps.content:
$b^2 < 256$ means $|b| < 16$, so $-16 < b < 16$. The largest positive integer satisfying $b < 16$ is $b = 15$.

The answer is 15.
